category_sources: source_facts.description says it tracks and controls custom shellz; official GitHub README says shellz manages ssh, telnet, kubernetes, winrm, web, and custom shells.
display_name_sources: source_facts.name is shellz; official GitHub README uses `shellz` as the project name.
docs_sources: Official GitHub README documents installation, identities, SSH, Telnet, WinRM, Kubernetes, SOCKS5, tunnels, plugins, and examples.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo already supplies https://github.com/evilsocket/shellz, so repo is returned as null per instruction.
tags_sources: Official GitHub README lists supported shell/session types including ssh, telnet, kubernetes, winrm, web, custom shells, SOCKS5, and reverse tunnels.
